Title: Verify age-appropriate vaccine schedule recommendation for an infant
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; pediatric patient aged 2 months exists with no prior immunizations
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Open the Immunizations module | 3. View the recommended schedule | 4. Inspect the recommended vaccines and due dates
Expected_Result: Schedule recommends 2-month series (e.g. DTaP, IPV, Hib, PCV13, RV, HepB) with correct due dates per the configured immunization schedule

Title: Verify next-due dose is recommended after recording a prior dose
Preconditions: Patient has DTaP dose 1 recorded at 2 months
Test_Steps: 1. Record DTaP dose 1 | 2. Reopen the schedule view | 3. Inspect the DTaP series state
Expected_Result: Series advances state from dose 1 to dose 2; next-due date computed using the minimum interval; dose 1 shown as administered

Title: Verify dose number auto-increments to the next sequential dose
Preconditions: Patient has Hib dose 1 and dose 2 recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Start a new Hib administration | 3. Observe the proposed dose number
Expected_Result: System proposes dose 3 as the next dose number for the Hib series

Title: Reject a duplicate dose number for the same vaccine series
Preconditions: Patient has DTaP dose 2 already recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Attempt to record DTaP dose 2 again with a new date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System warns of a duplicate dose number and blocks or requires explicit override with reason; no silent duplicate is created

Title: Boundary: record the final dose in a finite series and confirm series completion
Preconditions: Patient has HepB dose 1 and dose 2 recorded (3-dose series)
Test_Steps: 1. Record HepB dose 3 | 2. View the series status
Expected_Result: Series is marked complete; no further HepB doses are recommended; completion date recorded

Title: Boundary: dose number below 1 is rejected
Preconditions: On the immunization entry form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Enter dose number 0 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation rejects dose number 0; minimum allowed dose is 1

Title: Verify recording a vaccine with a valid lot number and non-expired expiry date
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse; patient with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Select vaccine and manufacturer | 3. Enter lot number and expiry date | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Immunization is saved with lot number, manufacturer and expiry captured and visible on the record

Title: Reject administration of a vaccine whose lot expiry date is in the past
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse; current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Select vaccine | 3. Enter an expired lot expiry date | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System warns the lot is expired and blocks administration or requires a documented override reason; safety warning is logged

Title: Boundary: lot expiry equal to administration date
Preconditions: Logged in as Nurse; current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a lot expiry equal to the administration date | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System treats end-of-day expiry consistently per policy; behaviour is deterministic and documented (accepted as still valid on the expiry day)

Title: Reject an empty or malformed lot number
Preconditions: On the immunization entry form
Test_Steps: 1. Select vaccine | 2. Leave lot number blank or enter invalid characters | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation requires a non-empty, format-valid lot number before save when manufacturer lot is mandatory

Title: Decision table: valid route and site combinations are accepted, invalid ones rejected
Preconditions: On the immunization entry form
Test_Steps: 1. For each row enter route and anatomical site | 2. Save | 3. Record outcome
Test_Data: R1 IM+left deltoid=valid; R2 SubQ+left thigh=valid; R3 Oral+deltoid=invalid; R4 IM+oral=invalid
Expected_Result: Valid clinically-coherent route/site pairs are accepted; mismatched pairs are warned or blocked per the decision table

Title: Verify route defaults correctly for an oral vaccine
Preconditions: On the immunization entry form
Test_Steps: 1. Select an oral vaccine (e.g. Rotavirus) | 2. Observe the route field
Expected_Result: Route defaults to Oral and site is not required or is set to not-applicable

Title: Pairwise: vaccine x route x site combinations
Preconditions: On the immunization entry form
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise set of vaccine/route/site | 2. Save each | 3. Verify acceptance
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination is processed per validation rules with consistent accept/reject outcomes

Title: Block or warn administration when a documented contraindication exists
Preconditions: Patient has a documented severe allergy to a vaccine component (e.g. neomycin) recorded in Allergies
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Select a vaccine containing the contraindicated component | 3. Attempt to administer
Expected_Result: System surfaces a contraindication alert before administration; requires override with documented clinical reason; alert is audited

Title: Warn on a live vaccine for an immunocompromised or pregnant patient
Preconditions: Patient flagged pregnant; live attenuated vaccine selected
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Select MMR (live vaccine) | 3. Attempt to administer
Expected_Result: Precaution/contraindication warning is shown for live vaccine in pregnancy; override requires documented justification

Title: Decision table: contraindication vs precaution handling
Preconditions: Patient records support multiple condition flags
Test_Steps: 1. Apply each condition/vaccine pair | 2. Attempt administration | 3. Record alert level
Test_Data: C1 anaphylaxis-to-prior-dose=contraindicated; C2 moderate-illness=precaution-defer; C3 mild-cold=administer; C4 egg-allergy+IIV=precaution
Expected_Result: Contraindications block/hard-warn, precautions soft-warn with defer guidance, non-issues allow administration

Title: Verify catch-up schedule is generated for a child behind on immunizations
Preconditions: Patient aged 18 months with only HepB dose 1 recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. View catch-up recommendations | 3. Inspect proposed sequence and intervals
Expected_Result: System generates a catch-up plan with appropriate minimum intervals and prioritized due vaccines

Title: Boundary: minimum interval enforcement between catch-up doses
Preconditions: Patient received DTaP dose 1 today
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to schedule DTaP dose 2 before the minimum interval | 2. Observe validation
Expected_Result: System flags the interval as too short and recommends the earliest valid date; invalid dose is warned or blocked

Title: Verify a dose given before the minimum age is flagged as invalid
Preconditions: Infant aged 5 weeks
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to record a vaccine with a minimum age of 6 weeks | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System warns the dose is below minimum age and may not count toward the series; invalid dose flagged

Title: Record a vaccine refusal with documented reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient on dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Select the offered vaccine | 3. Mark as refused/declined | 4. Enter reason and save
Expected_Result: Refusal is recorded with reason, date, and provider; status shown as not-done/refused; no administered dose is created

Title: Verify a refused vaccine remains on the due/recommended list
Preconditions: Patient refused influenza vaccine
Test_Steps: 1. Record refusal | 2. Reopen the schedule | 3. Inspect recommendation state
Expected_Result: Vaccine remains visible as due/offered with a refusal flag so it can be re-offered at the next visit

Title: Reject a refusal record without a reason when reason is mandatory
Preconditions: Refusal reason configured mandatory
Test_Steps: 1. Mark vaccine refused | 2. Leave reason blank | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation requires a refusal reason before save

Title: Record an adverse event following immunization (AEFI)
Preconditions: Patient has a recorded immunization administration
Test_Steps: 1. Open the administered immunization | 2. Add an adverse event/reaction | 3. Enter onset, severity, description | 4. Save
Expected_Result: AEFI is recorded and linked to the specific immunization and lot; appears in the patient reaction history

Title: Verify a severe AEFI triggers a contraindication on subsequent doses
Preconditions: Patient had anaphylaxis recorded after DTaP dose 1
Test_Steps: 1. Record severe AEFI for DTaP dose 1 | 2. Attempt DTaP dose 2 | 3. Observe alert
Expected_Result: Subsequent same-vaccine administration is contraindicated/hard-warned based on the prior severe reaction

Title: Verify AEFI report can be exported for regulatory submission (VAERS-style)
Preconditions: An AEFI record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the AEFI record | 2. Generate the regulatory report/export | 3. Inspect required fields
Expected_Result: Export contains required reporting fields (patient demographics, vaccine, lot, reaction, onset) for regulatory submission

Title: Verify immunization record is reportable to an external immunization registry (IIS)
Preconditions: Registry/IIS integration configured; immunization recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record an immunization | 2. Trigger registry submission | 3. Verify acknowledgement
Expected_Result: Immunization is transmitted to the registry and an acknowledgement/accept message is received and logged

Title: Verify an immunization coverage report by antigen and age cohort
Preconditions: Multiple patients with immunization records exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open Reporting | 2. Run immunization coverage report | 3. Filter by antigen and age cohort
Expected_Result: Report returns accurate coverage rates (numerator/denominator) for the selected antigen and cohort

Title: Create a FHIR Immunization resource via the API
Preconditions: FHIR API reachable; OAuth/Basic auth valid; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST an Immunization resource | 2. Read the response | 3. GET the created resource
Expected_Result: Resource is created (201) with a server id; status, vaccineCode (CVX), patient reference and occurrence persist on read-back

Title: Validate required FHIR Immunization elements are enforced
Preconditions: FHIR API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. POST an Immunization missing status | 2. POST missing vaccineCode | 3. POST missing patient | 4. Inspect each response
Expected_Result: Each invalid request returns 400/422 with an OperationOutcome identifying the missing required element

Title: Verify vaccineCode uses a valid CVX/SNOMED code system URI
Preconditions: FHIR API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. POST an Immunization with vaccineCode system http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx | 2. POST one with an invalid system URI | 3. Compare
Expected_Result: Valid CVX system URI is accepted; invalid/unknown code system is rejected or flagged per terminology validation

Title: Search FHIR Immunization by patient and date
Preconditions: Immunizations exist for the patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET Immunization?patient=Patient/123&date=ge2026-01-01 | 2. Inspect the Bundle
Expected_Result: Search returns a Bundle of matching Immunization resources scoped to the patient and date range only

Title: Verify status notDone/entered-in-error transitions on a FHIR Immunization
Preconditions: An Immunization resource exists with status completed
Test_Steps: 1. PUT status entered-in-error | 2. GET resource | 3. Verify status and statusReason
Expected_Result: Status transition persists; resource is excluded from active immunization counts; prior state is auditable

Title: Process an inbound HL7 v2 VXU vaccine update message
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured; inbound VXU feed enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send a VXU^V04 message with RXA segment | 2. Process | 3. Verify the immunization record
Expected_Result: RXA segment is parsed into an immunization with correct vaccine, lot and date; ACK (AA) returned

Title: Verify a malformed RXA segment produces a negative acknowledgement
Preconditions: HL7 inbound feed enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send a VXU with a malformed/incomplete RXA | 2. Observe ACK
Expected_Result: Interface returns AE/AR negative acknowledgement with an error describing the missing required field; no partial record created

Title: Query immunization history via HL7 QBP/RSP (registry query)
Preconditions: Registry query interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send a QBP^Q11 immunization history query | 2. Receive RSP | 3. Inspect returned doses
Expected_Result: RSP returns the patient immunization history with correct CVX-coded doses and dates

Title: Verify only authorized roles can administer or edit immunizations
Preconditions: Roles: Nurse (allowed), Receptionist (not allowed) exist
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as Receptionist | 2. Attempt to record an immunization | 3. Log in as Nurse and retry
Expected_Result: Receptionist is denied (privilege check); Nurse succeeds; authorization decision is enforced server-side

Title: Verify immunization changes are written to the audit log
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Record an immunization | 2. Edit it | 3. Void it | 4. Inspect the audit trail
Expected_Result: Create, update and void actions each produce audit entries capturing user, timestamp, and changed fields

Title: Verify IDOR protection on the FHIR Immunization endpoint
Preconditions: Two patients with immunizations; user scoped to patient A
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as a user scoped to patient A | 2. GET an Immunization belonging to patient B by id | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Access to another patient's immunization is denied (403/404); no PHI leakage across patients

Title: Verify voiding an immunization is a soft delete preserving history
Preconditions: DB read access; an immunization record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Void the immunization in the UI | 2. Query the obs/immunization table | 3. Inspect voided flags
Expected_Result: Record is soft-deleted (voided=true with voided_by, date_voided, void_reason); row is retained for audit, not physically removed

Title: Verify lot number and expiry persist as discrete coded/structured fields
Preconditions: DB read access; immunization with lot recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record an immunization with lot and expiry | 2. Query the backing tables | 3. Inspect stored values
Expected_Result: Lot number, manufacturer and expiry are stored as discrete retrievable fields, not concatenated free text

Title: Verify referential integrity between immunization, patient and provider
Preconditions: DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Record an immunization | 2. Query foreign keys to patient and provider | 3. Validate references resolve
Expected_Result: Immunization row references valid patient and provider/encounter ids; no orphaned references

Title: Verify immunization entry form is keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Immunization entry form open; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all fields | 2. Operate dropdowns and date pickers via keyboard | 3. Submit with Enter
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard in a logical order; focus is visible; no keyboard trap

Title: Verify contraindication alerts are announced to screen readers
Preconditions: Screen reader active; contraindication scenario set up
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a contraindication alert | 2. Listen to the screen reader announcement
Expected_Result: The alert is announced via an ARIA live region (role=alert) so a non-sighted clinician is warned before administering

Title: Verify combination vaccine satisfies multiple antigen requirements
Preconditions: Schedule supports combination vaccines
Test_Steps: 1. Record a combination vaccine (e.g. DTaP-IPV-Hib) | 2. View the schedule for each constituent antigen
Expected_Result: Each constituent antigen series is advanced appropriately by the single combination administration

Title: Boundary: AEFI onset time of zero/immediate vs delayed days
Preconditions: Immunization record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Record AEFI with onset 0 minutes | 2. Record another with onset 21 days | 3. Save both
Expected_Result: Both immediate and delayed onset values are accepted and stored accurately without rounding errors

Title: Record a historical immunization administered elsewhere without lot detail
Preconditions: Patient on dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open Immunizations | 2. Add a historical/external dose | 3. Mark source as historical | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Historical dose is recorded with a source indicator; lot/site optional for historical; still counts toward the series

Title: Verify future-dated administration date is rejected
Preconditions: Current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an administration date in the future | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects a future administration date for an administered dose; validation error shown

Title: Pairwise: schedule recommendation across age x prior-doses x risk-condition
Preconditions: Patients configured for combinations
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination open the schedule | 2. Compare recommended vaccines
Expected_Result: Recommendations correctly reflect each combination including risk-based vaccines (e.g. pneumococcal for asplenia)

Title: State transition: refused then later administered at a follow-up visit
Preconditions: Vaccine previously refused
Test_Steps: 1. View refused vaccine | 2. At a new visit administer the dose | 3. View status history
Expected_Result: Status transitions from refused to administered; both events retained in history with dates

Title: Verify temporary precaution (acute moderate illness) defers but does not block
Preconditions: Patient flagged with acute moderate febrile illness
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to administer a non-live vaccine | 2. Observe guidance
Expected_Result: System shows a defer/precaution recommendation but allows administration with documented clinical judgement

Title: Verify FHIR Immunization includes lotNumber and expirationDate
Preconditions: FHIR API reachable
Test_Steps: 1. POST an Immunization with lotNumber and expirationDate | 2. GET it | 3. Inspect fields
Expected_Result: lotNumber and expirationDate persist and are returned on read; map correctly to source fields

Title: Verify FHIR Immunization reaction references an observed reaction
Preconditions: FHIR API reachable; AEFI captured
Test_Steps: 1. GET an Immunization with a recorded reaction | 2. Inspect the reaction element
Expected_Result: Immunization.reaction is populated with detail/date/reported referencing the adverse event accurately

Title: Verify site rotation guidance when two injections share a visit
Preconditions: Two injectable vaccines to be given at one visit
Test_Steps: 1. Record vaccine 1 at left deltoid | 2. Record vaccine 2 same visit | 3. Observe site guidance
Expected_Result: System suggests a different anatomical site for the second injection to avoid overlap

Title: Verify de-duplication when the same dose arrives from registry and local entry
Preconditions: Registry integration active; a dose recorded locally
Test_Steps: 1. Record a dose locally | 2. Receive the same dose from the registry | 3. Inspect the record list
Expected_Result: System de-duplicates the matching dose (no double counting); a single reconciled record remains

Title: Verify PHI in immunization export is restricted to authorized consumers
Preconditions: Export/API endpoint with auth
Test_Steps: 1. Request an immunization export without valid token | 2. Request with a valid scoped token | 3. Compare
Expected_Result: Unauthenticated request is rejected (401); authorized request returns only permitted PHI per scope

Title: Exploratory: rapid sequential entry and edit of multiple immunizations
Preconditions: Clinician on the immunization module
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly add several immunizations | 2. Edit and reorder | 3. Void one | 4. Observe consistency
Expected_Result: No data loss, mis-association, or stale UI state; each record retains correct vaccine/dose/lot; counts remain accurate

Title: Exploratory: timezone and date boundary around midnight for administration date
Preconditions: Server and client in different timezones
Test_Steps: 1. Record an immunization near local midnight | 2. Read back via UI and FHIR | 3. Compare dates
Expected_Result: Administration date is consistent and not shifted across day boundaries between UI and API representations

Title: Decision table: dose validity by interval and age combinations
Preconditions: Series rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Apply each combination of interval-met and age-met | 2. Record dose | 3. Capture validity
Test_Data: D1 age-ok+interval-ok=valid; D2 age-ok+interval-short=invalid; D3 age-low+interval-ok=invalid; D4 both-fail=invalid
Expected_Result: Only doses meeting both minimum age and minimum interval are counted as valid; others flagged invalid

Title: Verify overdue vaccines are highlighted distinctly from upcoming due
Preconditions: Patient with one overdue and one upcoming vaccine
Test_Steps: 1. Open the schedule view | 2. Inspect status indicators
Expected_Result: Overdue vaccines are visually distinguished (e.g. overdue badge) from those merely upcoming; not relying on color alone

Title: Verify a reported AEFI generates a patient-safety notification to the provider
Preconditions: Notification subsystem configured
Test_Steps: 1. Record a serious AEFI | 2. Verify provider notification is generated
Expected_Result: A patient-safety notification/alert is generated to the responsible provider and logged

Title: Pairwise: manufacturer x lot x vaccine consistency check
Preconditions: On the immunization entry form
Test_Steps: 1. Enter pairwise manufacturer/lot/vaccine combinations | 2. Save each | 3. Validate
Expected_Result: System validates manufacturer is consistent with the selected vaccine and flags expired lots across combinations

Title: Verify CVX-to-vaccine mapping correctness on inbound VXU
Preconditions: HL7 inbound feed enabled; CVX mapping table loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Send VXU with CVX 20 | 2. Verify mapped vaccine | 3. Send VXU with an unknown CVX
Expected_Result: Known CVX maps to the correct vaccine concept; unknown CVX is rejected or routed to an exception queue

Title: Verify schedule recalculates after a historical dose is back-entered
Preconditions: Catch-up plan generated for a behind-schedule child
Test_Steps: 1. View current catch-up plan | 2. Back-enter a historical dose | 3. Re-view the plan
Expected_Result: Catch-up plan recalculates to reflect the back-entered dose, removing it from the due list

Title: Verify immunization parity through the Resource Adapter Layer across backends
Preconditions: Adapter routes to OpenMRS and a second FHIR backend; patient exists in both
Test_Steps: 1. Create an immunization via the adapter for each backend | 2. Read back via the adapter | 3. Compare normalized output
Expected_Result: Both backends accept the immunization and the adapter returns equivalent normalized data (CVX/status consistent)

Title: Verify override of a contraindication requires reason and is fully audited
Preconditions: Contraindication alert configured; clinician with override privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a contraindication | 2. Override without a reason | 3. Override with a reason | 4. Inspect audit
Expected_Result: Override without reason is blocked; override with reason proceeds and records user, reason and timestamp in the audit log
